text.title: AI Feature UX Pattern Library Decision Tree

root-question.text: Does the feature have user-facing UI surfaces (not just backend pipeline)?

question.text: Can the model refuse or return low-confidence outputs in this feature?
Can the AI-driven action be irreversible for the user (send, delete, publish, charge)?

branch.conclusion: Mandate full UX set: confidence indicator + refusal surface + edit-handoff + why-trail + undo gate. Optional adds: progressive disclosure, batch undo.
Mandate core set: confidence indicator + refusal surface + edit-handoff. Undo gate optional.
Light set: confidence-light indicator + edit-handoff. Refusal surface unnecessary.
Skip — this methodology is for user-facing AI; backend pipelines don't need UX patterns.

example.p: ui_surface=true → refusal possible=true → irreversible=true (publish action) → conclusion: mandate full set including undo gate.
